title: Northgate Dental Congress 2027: delegate name-badge data-merge from roster CSV into authored InDesign template, print PDF + per-badge proof
one_line_ask: Take our approved event wordmark, delegate roster CSV (~1,000 rows) and authored InDesign badge template, prep and vectorize the wordmark plus the four role QR marks, then run an InDesign data-merge to bind every delegate into a personalised name badge and export a print-ready imposed PDF plus a high-res proof.

issues: Background is off-white instead of transparent (should be transparent PNG per brief)
Slightly oversized and off-centre crop is correct per brief
No gradients, mockups, or real-world brands present
Logo is centred rather than deliberately off-centre/oversized as specified in brief, reducing the 'wrong crop' realism intended
Background is a very pale warm off-white rather than fully transparent PNG — acceptable as source asset but worth noting for the designer
Tooth icon orbital ring is slightly thin relative to the tooth body, may need weight adjustment at vectorisation stage
Slight photographic perspective/angle rather than pure flat scan — card is propped at a very slight angle introducing minimal keystoning, acceptable as source input
Plant and pot visible in background — minor distraction but does not clutter main subject
Image appears to be a photograph of a printed card rather than a direct digital export, meaning slight luminance noise and shadow at base are present — within acceptable source-file tolerance

The input image is perfectly square (1000x1000px), whereas the prompt specified 'exported slightly non-square (e.g. 700x640px)'. However, this is a minor deviation and the content itself is square, which aligns with the goal of normalizing QRs to a square frame ratio.
Slight perspective/tilt present (within acceptable <=5deg threshold for source asset)
Physical print card photographed rather than flat digital export — acceptable as source input
Minor luminance noise in background area — designer fix expected
Bottom edge shows slight shadow from physical prop stand — minor
Top-right finder pattern appears slightly clipped at the card edge — minor crop tightness
No speaker name or event text below QR, but brief does not require it
Lanyard hole and cord visible — acceptable real-world prop, not a defect
QR code appears slightly damaged/noisy in center modules which may affect scannability, but acceptable as source asset
Card appears very slightly non-square as intended
Small hole punch at top center is present and appropriate for a badge/lanyard credential

assets.name: badge_template.indd
assets.note: Client-provided .indd template; represented here as an authored text proxy (binary Adobe format not synthesizable).
